sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.62 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group such that every${ Aut\,} G$fixes every conjugacy class of$G$(setwise). Must${ Aut\,} G ={ Inn\,}G$? { D.\,MacHale} Not necessarily: the paper (H.\,Heineken, Arch. Math.$($Basel\/$)$, 33 (1979/80), no.\,6, 497--503), in particular, produces finite$p$-groups all of whose automorphisms preserve all the conjugacy classes, while by Gasch\"utz' theorem every finite$p$-group has outer au...
